title: campanula versicolor subsp. tenorei
intro: Campanula versicolor subsp. tenorei, also known as Tenore's versicolor bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and possessing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Campanula versicolor subsp. tenorei is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
